title: Spermacoce macrantha
intro: Spermacoce macrantha is a flowering plant species, commonly known as tall bearded bindweed or tall skeleton weed, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant is known for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Spermacoce macrantha include alkaloids, glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Spermacoce macrantha is used in various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Spermacoce macrantha is classified as a species within the genus Spermacoce and the family Rubiaceae. This classification places it within the order Gentianales. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The genus Spermacoce is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. Spermacoce macrantha is a species of flowering plant that is native to certain regions and is often found in warm and humid environments.
